title: Deep Financial Forensics Through Cross-Referenced Obras Publicas Graph
methodology: Traced money flows through 43,615 SAME_ENTITY cross-references linking contractors, donors, officials, asset declarations, and companies

identical_contract_amounts.note: Three distinct contractors each received exactly $50,568,000 in single contracts — strong indicator of coordinated bidding or contract splitting

stale_planned_projects.note: Over $703 billion ARS frozen in planned mega-projects with negligible execution

offshore_chains.path_tested: Contractor -> SAME_ENTITY -> Company -> OFFICER_OF_COMPANY -> CompanyOfficer -> SAME_ENTITY -> OffshoreOfficer
offshore_chains.note: No direct offshore chains found through this path. Offshore connections may exist through longer paths or different relationship types.

pay_to_play_indicators: Three distinct contractors received the exact sum of $50,568,000 in single contracts — indicates bid rigging or pre-arranged allocation
Government officials (Ministry Director, Nuclear Head, Senator) registered as contractors — direct conflicts of interest
Officials show exponential asset growth coinciding with contract awards — suggests illicit enrichment
shell_entity_patterns: Government officials holding CUITs to operate as contractors blurs state authority and private contracting
Use of SA entities alongside individual names suggests network designed to obscure beneficial ownership
Trajtenberg asset growth ($26K to $1.03B) and Olivera ($6.6M to $26M) indicate potential money laundering
budget_red_flags: 15 mega-projects exceeding $10B each with under 10% execution — implies ghost projects, inflated costs, or deliberate stalling
Santiago del Estero and Santa Cruz at 55% execution — significantly below national average
Combination of high-value contracts and low execution rates indicates systemic waste
investigation_priorities: Trace origin of identical $50,568,000 figure to determine if fixed quota or manipulated pricing
Forensic asset tracking for Trajtenberg, Scolari, and Olivera to link growth to specific contract awards
Physical audits of 15 under-executed mega-projects to confirm existence and progress
Suspend and audit contracting authority of officials holding dual roles

summary.highest_asset_growth: TRAJTENBERG LUIS ALBERTO — 6,521% over 9 years ($26K to $1.03B)
